concerns.text: Tasks T043, T044, T046, and T050 (Phase 6) are marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depend on T031 (Phase 2) for the 'measurement_uncertainty_status' and 'quantity_of_substance_status' flags. T031 must complete before these tasks can correctly determine if data is 'Not Available'. Marking them [P] implies they can run before T031, which would cause them to fail or produce incorrect 'Not Available' flags based on missing context.

Task T050 and T051 are marked [P] but list dependencies on T014.5 and T020.1 (Phase 3/4). The [P] tag suggests they can run in parallel with US1/US2 implementation, but they explicitly require the *output artifacts* (baseline_test_predictions.csv, rf_test_predictions.csv) from those completed tasks. This is a hidden ordering violation; they must be sequential to the completion of T014.5 and T020.1, not parallel to the implementation of US1/US2.

Task T033 (Conformational Limitation Report) is marked [P] but requires 'deviation_magnitude' (absolute residual difference). This metric is only available after T014.5 (Baseline) and T020.1 (RF Evaluation) are complete. The [P] tag is incorrect as it cannot run until the residual data is generated in Phase 3/4.

Tasks T045 and T047 are marked [P] but depend on T020.1 (RF Test Evaluation) to compare predictions against the 'NumRotatableBonds > 10' heuristic. They cannot execute in parallel with the RF training (T020) or baseline generation (T014) because they require the final prediction residuals. The [P] tag is misleading and violates the producer-consumer flow.

Task T031 is marked [P] but depends on T008 (Data Download). While T008 is also marked [P], T031 explicitly states 'Must wait for T008 completion'. The [P] tag on T031 is technically correct relative to *other* Phase 2 tasks, but the dependency on T008 (which is also [P]) creates a sequential chain within the 'parallel' phase. This is a minor semantic nit, but the [P] tag on T031 might confuse the scheduler into thinking it can start before T008 finishes if T008 is delayed.
